title: VG-CoT: Towards Trustworthy Visual Reasoning via Grounded Chain-of-Thought

authors.name: banggeuk
authors.affiliation: Department of Artificial Intelligence, Chung-Ang University

authors.name: khyun8072
authors.affiliation: Department of Artificial Intelligence, Chung-Ang University

authors.affiliation: Department of Artificial Intelligence, Chung-Ang University

abstract: The paper discusses Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) and their limitations in local region-based reasoning. Current vision-language datasets focus on image-level understanding rather than fine-grained reasoning. Manual annotation for datasets with local region information is costly and not scalable. Existing multimodal Chain-of-Thought (CoT) datasets don't explicitly link reasoning to visual evidence. The paper proposes the Visual Grounding Chain-of-Thought (VG-CoT) dataset to address these issues.
key_contributions: VG-CoT dataset that explicitly aligns visual evidence with reasoning processes
Supports scalable and efficient learning of complex, local region-based reasoning
Enables verification of whether model logic is faithful to actual image evidence
Provides foundation for measuring not just answer accuracy but also rationale accuracy and logical soundness
problem_statement: LVLMs have limitations in local region-based reasoning
Current datasets don't support fine-grained reasoning based on detailed attributes or object relationships
Manual annotation for region-based datasets is costly and not scalable
Existing CoT datasets don't link reasoning to specific visual evidence
solution: VG-CoT dataset that explicitly aligns visual evidence with reasoning processes
